notes:
vân vân means “and so on” or “etc.” It’s used to show the list continues, but you don’t want to say everything.

It comes at the end of a list, after commas. You use it when the other items are similar or obvious.

In writing, you’ll sometimes see it shortened as v.v. It’s neutral and works in both speaking and writing.

example.es: Podemos ir al parque, al cine, etc.
example.en: We can go to the park, the cinema, and so on.
example.vi: Chúng ta có thể đi đến công viên, rạp chiếu phim, vân vân.

exampleSentences: Em thích ăn phở, bún bò, bánh mì, vân vân khi đi du lịch.
Trong lớp học, chúng ta học về toán, lý, hóa, vân vân.
Chị ấy sưu tầm tem, đồng xu, bưu thiếp, vân vân để giải trí.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I like to eat pho, beef noodle soup, banh mi, and so on when traveling.
In class, we study math, physics, chemistry, and so on.
She collects stamps, coins, postcards, and so on for fun.
notes_md:
**vân vân** means “and so on” or “etc.” It’s used to show the list continues, but you don’t want to say everything.

It comes at the **end of a list**, after commas. You use it when the other items are similar or obvious.


In writing, you’ll sometimes see it shortened as **v.v.** It’s neutral and works in both speaking and writing.

content.sentence: Cô ấy thích đọc sách, báo, ___.

translation.en: She likes to read books, newspapers, etc.
We talk about etc. and other things.

content.sentence: Chúng tôi nói về ___ và những thứ khác.
